title: SSC CGL 2026 Notification Expected in April — 15,000+ Vacancies for Graduates
author: Ashutosh Khulbe
excerpt: The SSC CGL 2026 notification was delayed from March 31 and is now expected in late April 2026. Here's what we know about vacancies, exam dates, and eligibility.
key_stat: 15,000+ expected vacancies across Group B and C posts

related_pages.title: Bank PO vs SSC CGL comparison

body: The Staff Selection Commission was scheduled to release the SSC CGL 2026 notification on March 31, 2026, but the release has been delayed. The notification is now expected in the last week of April 2026, with the Tier 1 exam scheduled for May–June 2026.

SSC CGL recruits graduates for Group B and Group C posts across central government departments including Income Tax, Customs, CBI, CBDT, and CBEC. Last year saw 15,118 vacancies filled through this exam, and experts predict a similar or higher number for 2026.

The exam follows a two-tier pattern. Tier 1 is qualifying in nature with 100 MCQs covering General Intelligence, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, English, and General Awareness. Tier 2 is the main scoring stage with advanced papers. Application fee is Rs 100 for general category, with fee exemptions for SC/ST and women candidates.

callout.heading: Eligibility at a glance
callout.content: Age: 18-32 years (relaxation for reserved categories). Qualification: Bachelor's degree from a recognized university. Some posts like JSO require Mathematics/Statistics background. No limit on number of attempts. Application fee: Rs 100 (exempted for SC/ST/women).

faqs.question: When will SSC CGL 2026 application form be available?
faqs.answer: The application form will be released along with the official notification on ssc.gov.in. Based on current expectations, registration should open in late April 2026 with the last date to apply in May 2026.

faqs.question: What is SSC CGL salary range?
faqs.answer: SSC CGL salary ranges from Pay Level 4 (Rs 25,500 basic) to Pay Level 7 (Rs 44,900 basic), depending on the post. With allowances (DA, HRA, TA), the total monthly salary ranges from Rs 40,000 to Rs 80,000+ depending on city and post.

faqs.question: Is SSC CGL Tier 1 qualifying?
faqs.answer: Yes. Tier 1 is qualifying in nature — it only determines whether you proceed to Tier 2. Your final merit ranking is based on Tier 2 marks. However, you need to score above the cutoff in each section of Tier 1 to qualify.
